title: AnswerResponse
description: Final answer returned by the backend generation layer (backend/app/generation/) to the frontend after LLM processing. Includes the generated answer, structured citations, the retrieval method used, and latency. Derived from vector_retrieval/src/utils/prompts.py and response_printer.py.

query.description: The original user query echoed back.
query.example: What are the main risks of RAG hallucination?

answer.description: The LLM-generated answer, grounded in the retrieved chunks. 2-4 sentences.
answer.example: The main risks of RAG hallucination include retrieval mismatch, incomplete context, and weak citation grounding.

evidence_used.description: Bullet-level breakdown of which chunks contributed what to the answer. Maps to the 'Evidence Used' section of the LLM prompt output.

chunk_id.description: The chunk ID that contributed evidence.

contribution.description: Brief description of what this chunk contributed to the answer.
contribution.example: Described retrieval mismatch as a primary hallucination risk.

citations.description: Structured citations for all chunks referenced in the answer.

retrieval_method.description: The retrieval method used to produce this answer.

latency_ms.description: Total end-to-end latency in milliseconds (retrieval + generation).

example.query: What are the main risks of RAG hallucination?
example.answer: The main risks of RAG hallucination include retrieval mismatch, incomplete context, and weak citation grounding.

evidence_used.contribution: Described retrieval mismatch as a primary hallucination risk.
